channel.title: My New Hugo Project
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Project

item.title: Reflection Blog on unit 3
item.description: Unit 3 was entitled " Reflecting on Failure." It was a bit different from other units in that the knowledge was gained directly through an activity on a Wiki project. Thus, instead of the usual classroom approach, we engaged with a real-world failure case to develop a shared resource. Therefore, the topic was more appealing and engaging. The case study assigned to our group was on the AT&T network outage. This demonstrated how a minor error in software could cause a massive failure in communication systems. It also shows how poor testing leads to significant failure. This again brought home the point that building reliable systems, fixing bugs properly, and designing technology with care should not be taken for granted, especially when a considerable number of people depend on it. Working on this case study, I realized that most technological failures result from several causes: poor assumptions, poor communication, and, as stated earlier, overconfidence. The reading from " Right Kind of Wrong: The Science of Failing Well" taught me many new things and allowed me to understand them better. It discussed how some failures are appropriate and necessary for learning. While this notion was quite attractive, it was hard to accept because failure is typically viewed as negative. I found this unit easier to comprehend because the concept of failure was better put across to me practically. Moreover, the Wiki made learning more fun and easier and changed my perspective on failure.

item.title: Reflection Blog of first two topics
item.description: We started with our first Topic called “What is Information?” and we learnt various thing throughtout this topic.In this unit we started with first exploring Data Information Knowledge Wisdom (DIKW) hierarchy a model that explains how raw facts are transformed into meaningful understanding and better decision making. At the base of the hierarchy is data. Data refers to raw unprocessed facts such as numbers measurements or observations. Just on their own these facts do not carry meaning. Example - a list of temperature numbers collected throughout the day is simply data. When data is organized/ processed it becomes information and information helps us understand what data represents. After that comes knowledge which gets developed when people interpret information using experience,learning and understanding. Finally at the top of all this comes Wisdom. Wisdom involves knowledge to make usefull and thoughtfull decisions. Overall the DIKW model explains how raw data can be turned into useful information then into knowledge and finally used to make good decisions. During the first unit we also had learnt to setup our Reflection Blog. After this unit ended we started our second unit called “The Nature of Information Problems” in which we learnt mant things such Brightness and contrast ,Geometric transformations in images,Optimization and formal analysis ,Modeling and abstraction etc. We started with the first topic that was introduced to us and it was the concept of images and pixels. In computing a digital image is usually represented as a 2d grid of pixels where each pixel stores information about color or brightness. Pixels are veru usefull and common with modern technology. Then we learnt about how digital images are represented in computers.Images are stored as numerical values often using RGB (red, green, blue) color components.Each color component usually ranges from 0 to 255 representing different intensities of light. In the next topic we explored brightness, contrast, and image histograms. These concepts help tp improve the visual quality of images. Brightness adjustments change the overall lightness of an image while contrast enhancement increases the difference between light and dark areas in an image. In the last we learnt about some geometric transformations which include things such as rotation, scaling reflection etc.These transformations change the shape or orientation of images while preserving their structure. These topics help us understand the basic ways digital images are created, changed and used by computers. At the last we also did bit of optimization and solved some optimization problems. Overall both the topics i learnt were new for me but personally i found topic 2 little challenging especially the concept of images and pixels since this was completly new to me. It took me a lot of reading and working to do to understand this topic and i finally managed to as well. Everything else i think i could relate to little bit as we had done parts of it earlier as well in School, for example most of the questions on optimization , geometric transformation just involved some amount of maths which we had already studied so that was something i was able to do easily compared to other topics.
